votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the fuel-based most significant digit extraction, the filtering logic, and the odd digit checks.
The Python code accurately implements the Coq specification, correctly translating the helper functions, the fuel-based most significant digit extraction, and the filtering logic.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, correctly implementing the fuel-based most significant digit extraction, the odd digit checks, and the filtering logic.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification. The key functions match: last_digit computes abs(n % 10), msd_fuel iteratively finds the most significant digit with fuel limiting iterations, special_number_b checks n > 10 (not abs_n > 10, matching Coq's '10 <? n'), and checks both first and last digits are odd. The specialFilter_impl counts matching elements. The iterative Python msd_fuel is equivalent to the recursive Coq version. Both pre and spec functions match the Coq definitions.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification. The key functions match: last_digit computes abs(n % 10), msd_fuel iteratively finds the most significant digit with the same logic (loop vs recursion), special_number_b checks n > 10, odd first digit, and odd last digit. The specialFilter_impl counts matching elements. While msd_fuel uses iteration instead of recursion, the behavior is equivalent for concrete inputs. The condition (10 <? n) in Coq and (10 < n) in Python both check if n is strictly greater than 10. All concrete computations will produce the same results.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification. The key functions match: last_digit computes abs(n % 10), msd_fuel iteratively finds the most significant digit with fuel limiting iterations, special_number_b checks n > 10 (not abs_n > 10, matching Coq's '10 <? n'), odd first digit, and odd last digit. The specialFilter_impl counts matching elements. The while-loop in Python's msd_fuel correctly simulates the recursive Coq version. Both pre and spec functions match their Coq counterparts.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it checks n > 10 (not abs), computes most-significant and last digits from abs(n) using equivalent msd_fuel and last_digit implementations, tests both for oddness, and counts matches. Concrete behavior aligns for all integer inputs.
Both compute count of n > 10 with odd first and last digits of |n|; msd_fuel and last_digit match Coq behavior on all integers, and the filter/count equals the Coq spec.

Both implementations count integers > 10 whose first and last digits (of abs value) are odd. msd_fuel behavior (using abs(n) as both fuel and value), digit/odd checks, and aggregation match Coq’s definitions on all integer inputs.
